symptoms.symptom_name: Acute sharp/tearing LUQ pain radiating to the left shoulder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute focal splenic pain with referred left-shoulder (diaphragmatic) component is characteristic of an infarct in a massive spleen.
Sudden severe pain over a fragile enlarged spleen is a sentinel for rupture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic left-upper-quadrant fullness and early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive LUQ fullness with early satiety from gastric compression is the hallmark symptom of massive splenomegaly.
Extramedullary haematopoiesis drives splenic enlargement in myelofibrosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Drenching sweats, fever and unintentional weight loss (B-symptoms)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional B-symptoms reflect the inflammatory cytokine burden of a myeloproliferative neoplasm.
Low-grade fever and sweats accompany the inflammatory response to an acute infarct.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked pallor and new spontaneous bruising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor indicates anaemia and spontaneous bruising indicates thrombocytopenia/coagulopathy from marrow replacement by fibrosis.
Bone pain reflects marrow expansion and fibrosis in a myeloproliferative process.
Patient-obtainable home reading corroborating fever; admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive firm nodular splenomegaly (12 cm below costal margin) with localised tenderness, voluntary guarding and a splenic friction rub

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A friction rub over an acutely tender massive spleen is highly specific for splenic infarction — a clinician palpation/auscultation finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatomegaly (4 cm below right costal margin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hepatomegaly accompanies splenic extramedullary haematopoiesis; a palpation finding unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 112), fever (38.2), tachypnoea (RR 22) on clinician vitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic inflammatory response to splenic infarction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician-measured vitals; the AI Doctor infers distress from the patient's report, not measured numbers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Leukoerythroblastic blood film with teardrop poikilocytes and anaemia

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count and peripheral blood film
investigation_details.expected_result: Leukoerythroblastic picture with teardrop poikilocytes; anaemia; variable platelet count
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying myeloproliferative neoplasm and cytopenias remain unrecognised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Teardrop cells and a leukoerythroblastic film are characteristic of marrow fibrosis; a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bone marrow trephine showing profound reticulin/collagen fibrosis

investigation_details.investigation_name: Bone marrow aspirate and trephine biopsy
investigation_details.expected_result: Marrow fibrosis with atypical megakaryocytes; molecular studies for JAK2/CALR/MPL
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Primary myelofibrosis cannot be confirmed or distinguished from CML.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marrow fibrosis on trephine is the definitive diagnostic finding; a hospital procedure the AI cannot perform.

investigation_details.expected_result: Wedge-shaped non-enhancing area(s) in the spleen consistent with infarction; assessment for capsular breach/rupture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Splenic infarction and impending rupture cannot be confirmed or excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging is confirmatory; unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Massive splenomegaly with B-symptoms and cytopenias
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic massive splenomegaly, constitutional B-symptoms, anaemia/bruising and bone pain together point to an underlying myeloproliferative neoplasm requiring urgent haematology work-up.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute splenic infarction on a fragile massive spleen
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Splenic infarction with risk of rupture
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden focal splenic pain with a friction rub, fever and tachycardia signals an acute infarct and impending rupture — the emergency driving disposition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-person assessment with CT abdomen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms/excludes splenic infarction and rupture; the immediate priority and beyond telehealth capability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and peripheral blood film
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses anaemia/thrombocytopenia before possible surgery and reveals the leukoerythroblastic picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bone marrow aspirate and trephine with molecular studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis of the underlying myeloproliferative neoplasm once the acute event is stabilised.
Prepares for haemorrhage risk from possible splenic rupture on a cytopenic background.
